nodes.label: MainActivity
nodes.description: The main entry point (Front Door) of the app

nodes.label: RushdulIlmApplication
nodes.description: The main entry point for the entire Android Application process

nodes.label: transcribeAudio (JNI)
nodes.description: JNI wrapper function to expose whisper.cpp STT to Kotlin

nodes.label: FatwaSourceConverter
nodes.description: Converts List of FatwaSources to JSON string and back for Room database storage

nodes.label: RushdulIlmDatabase
nodes.description: The main Room Database class that ties all entities and DAOs together

nodes.label: SavedAnswer
nodes.description: Defines the table structure for saving fatwa answers in the local database

nodes.label: SavedAnswerDao
nodes.description: Data Access Object (DAO) that defines database operations for saved answers

nodes.label: ApiService
nodes.description: Defines the Retrofit interface for the backend API

nodes.label: QueryRequest
nodes.description: Request payload model containing question and settings filters

nodes.label: QueryResponse
nodes.description: Response payload model containing RAG search results

nodes.label: AnswerHistoryRepository
nodes.description: Mediates between the ViewModel and the Room database for saving and retrieving answers

nodes.label: MainRepository
nodes.description: The central data manager that coordinates between network (Retrofit) and local storage

nodes.label: UserPreferencesRepository
nodes.description: A central storage for user-selected settings that need to be shared across multiple screens

nodes.label: DatabaseModule
nodes.description: Hilt Module to provide Room database and DAO instances to the app

nodes.label: NetworkModule
nodes.description: Provides Retrofit and OkHttp instances using Hilt Dependency Injection

nodes.label: FatwaAnswer
nodes.description: Layout for fatwa answers received from the RAG search pipeline

nodes.label: FatwaSource
nodes.description: Representing a single cited web reference website

nodes.label: AppLanguage
nodes.description: Defines every app language in one modular place

nodes.label: LanguageSelector
nodes.description: A dropdown menu allowing the user to select their preferred language for questions and answers.
nodes.rationale: Dropdown menus in Compose use ExposedDropdownMenuBox. An OutlinedTextField acts as the anchor read-only display, and ExposedDropdownMenu renders the list of choices when clicked.

nodes.label: MicButton
nodes.description: A reusable, animated microphone button for capturing user questions.
nodes.rationale: By abstracting the MicButton layout, we can use it on different screens without rewriting the layout or pulse animation code.

nodes.label: SourceSelector
nodes.description: Allows the user to pick which Islamic database to query.
nodes.rationale: LazyRow implements a horizontally scrolling layout list. Unlike a Row that constructs all its items at once, LazyRow is highly optimized, building chips only when visible.

nodes.label: VideoCard
nodes.description: Displays a single video entry in the video library list.
nodes.rationale: Cards are modular container widgets. We use semantics modifiers to merge all children texts into a single accessibility readout, so blind users can select the full card instead of struggling with tiny text fragments.

nodes.label: AnswerScreen
nodes.description: Displays the Islamic answer, source URL, and the 'Read Aloud' button.
nodes.rationale: State Flow representation binds view actions to ViewModel states asynchronously. The UI draws layout views depending entirely on the active State data.

nodes.label: AnswerVideoCard
nodes.description: Reusable UI card for related videos in AnswerScreen.
nodes.rationale: Reusable UI cards isolate UI widget styles from parent page frameworks. Making layout sub-elements separate functions increases readability and testability.

nodes.label: AnswersHistoryScreen
nodes.description: Displays a list of all previously saved answers.

nodes.label: SavedAnswerCard
nodes.description: Card component displaying a single saved answer.

nodes.label: HomeScreen
nodes.description: The main screen where the user asks questions using the mic.
nodes.rationale: Composable functions are the building blocks of Jetpack Compose. They take data from StateFlow streams and draw UI elements on the screen. Whenever state changes, the function recomposes automatically.

nodes.label: RushdulIlmNavGraph
nodes.description: The 'Traffic Controller' of our app - it connects all our screens together.
nodes.rationale: Navigation in Jetpack Compose uses a NavController to trigger screen changes, and a NavHost to draw the Composable screen linked to the active route.

nodes.label: Routes
nodes.description: Defines all the navigation 'addresses' (routes) for the app in one place.
nodes.rationale: Routes is a central registry containing the names of all screens in the app. By centralizing these strings, we ensure that navigating to a screen always uses the correct address.

nodes.label: SettingsScreen
nodes.description: Displays the app settings and offline download options.
nodes.rationale: User preference management binds UI controls to local repository storage. Changes selected in this screen apply globally across the application.

nodes.label: SettingsSectionTitle
nodes.description: Reusable UI header for settings sections.
nodes.rationale: Reusable UI headers isolate title font properties from parent screen lists. Isolating styling into clean sub-components makes screens look highly uniform.

nodes.label: SettingsRadioButton
nodes.description: Custom radio button row for settings.
nodes.rationale: Custom radio button rows increase accessibility touch targets. Selecting any portion of the horizontal row registers the click event.

nodes.label: SettingsSwitch
nodes.description: Custom toggle switch row for settings.
nodes.rationale: Custom toggle switch rows align toggles along screen edges automatically. It structures parameters to isolate labels from status controls.

nodes.label: DownloadItem
nodes.description: Widget organizing technical file size data inside structured cards for downloads.
nodes.rationale: Download widgets organize technical file size data inside structured cards. Organizing details inside cards helps users distinguish database download packages clearly.

nodes.label: VideoLibraryScreen
nodes.description: Displays a list of offline Islamic video lectures that the user can play.
nodes.rationale: Search query filtering processes input string modifications asynchronously. Updating search text flows triggers search logic in the background, updating list items.

nodes.label: IslamicGreen
nodes.description: Primary brand dark green color for primary buttons and success banners.

nodes.label: QuranicBlue
nodes.description: Accent calm blue color for clickable links and citations.

nodes.label: OfflineOrange
nodes.description: Bright orange color for the offline status banner.

nodes.label: ErrorRed
nodes.description: Error red color for warning states and network connection errors.

nodes.label: BackgroundCream
nodes.description: Soft warm yellow/white color for screen backgrounds.

nodes.label: TextPrimary
nodes.description: Soft black/grey color for high contrast readable text.

nodes.label: RushdulIlmTheme
nodes.description: Wraps the colors and typography into a complete Material3 Theme for the app.
nodes.rationale: A Theme coordinates a unified design system. We map our custom colors to standard semantic roles so standard Compose buttons and screens style themselves automatically.

nodes.label: Typography
nodes.description: Defines the text styles (fonts, sizes, weights) used across the app.
nodes.rationale: Material3 Typography maps common text roles to specific TextStyles. Declaring sizes in scale-independent pixels ensures accessibility settings are honored.

nodes.label: AudioRecorderHelper
nodes.description: Captures raw audio from the microphone at 16kHz mono for whisper.cpp processing.
nodes.rationale: AudioRecord is Android's lowest-level audio capture API. Whisper AI requires raw audio (PCM) format at 16,000 Hz.

nodes.label: NetworkTier
nodes.description: Enum defining the three possible connection states of the app.
nodes.rationale: Tier 1 is INTERNET (use cloud APIs), Tier 2 is LAN (use local Ubuntu server), Tier 3 is OFFLINE (use on-device models).

nodes.label: pingServer
nodes.description: Tries to open a quick connection to a specific IP and port to see if the server is alive.
nodes.rationale: Pinging a server returns true if successful within the timeout, false otherwise.

nodes.label: detectNetworkTier
nodes.description: Uses the Android system to check for active networks, and pings specific local server to determine the tier.
nodes.rationale: Since pinging involves a network call, this function should generally be run on a background thread to avoid freezing the UI.

nodes.label: observeNetworkTier
nodes.description: Creates a continuous stream (Flow) of network status updates by listening to Android's ConnectivityManager.
nodes.rationale: It listens to Android's ConnectivityManager for network changes and re-evaluates the tier automatically in real-time.

nodes.label: Resource
nodes.description: A generic wrapper class for handling UI states (Success, Error, Loading).
nodes.rationale: A sealed class is a closed hierarchy class. It restricts subclasses to be defined only within this file. It is perfect for representing state states that are mutually exclusive.

nodes.label: WhisperHelper
nodes.description: Kotlin interface to communicate with the native whisper.cpp C++ code.
nodes.rationale: This class acts as our Kotlin-side bridge to the C++ code. We use the 'external' keyword to tell Kotlin that the function implementation exists in the C++ library we loaded.

nodes.label: AnswerViewModel
nodes.description: The 'brain' of the Answer Screen. It holds the fatwa answer and related videos.
nodes.rationale: A ViewModel manages state for a specific screen, preserving data during screen rotation. We launch coroutines in viewModelScope to collect StateFlow streams from our Repository.

nodes.label: AnswersHistoryViewModel
nodes.description: Manages the state of the Answers History screen by fetching data from the local DB.

nodes.label: HomeViewModel
nodes.description: The brain of the Home Screen. Remembers choices and handles actions like tapping the mic.
nodes.rationale: Binds Home UI components to repositories.

nodes.label: SettingsViewModel
nodes.description: Manages the state of the Settings Screen.
nodes.rationale: Coordinates preferences state for the Settings screen.

nodes.label: VideoLibraryViewModel
nodes.description: Manages the state and search filtering for the Video Library screen.

nodes.label: ViewModels.kt
nodes.description: Placeholder to keep the viewmodel package visible.

nodes.label: build.gradle.kts
nodes.description: Configures tools and versions used across the entire project.

nodes.label: settings.gradle.kts
nodes.description: Defines the project name and where to find libraries.

nodes.label: download_indictrans2.py
nodes.description: Downloads the IndicTrans2 models from HuggingFace.

nodes.label: download_model.py
nodes.description: Downloads paraphrase-multilingual-MiniLM-L12-v2 model.

nodes.label: download_qwen3_embed.py
nodes.description: Downloads Qwen3-Embedding-0.6B locally.

nodes.label: download_tts.py
nodes.description: Downloads the indic-parler-tts model from Hugging Face.

nodes.label: fastapi_server.py
nodes.description: Main backend server for the Android app. Exposes REST endpoints.

nodes.label: QueryRequest
nodes.description: Pydantic model defining the shape of data expected from the Android app for querying.

nodes.label: ingest_deoband.py
nodes.description: Converts Deoband SQLite fatwas into vector embeddings and saves them in Qdrant.

nodes.label: ingest_islamqa.py
nodes.description: Converts IslamQA SQLite fatwas into vector embeddings and saves them in Qdrant.

nodes.label: qdrant_test_query.py
nodes.description: Tests qdrant queries and ingests data.

nodes.label: RagPipeline
nodes.description: Implements the Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipeline using LlamaIndex.
nodes.rationale: Binds data retrieval steps directly into the prompting window of the language model.

nodes.label: CombineQAPostprocessor
nodes.description: Custom post-processor that modifies retrieved text chunks.
nodes.rationale: Combines metadata payload fields (like separate question/answer fields) into a single text block.

nodes.label: MultiCollectionRetriever
nodes.description: Custom retriever that bypasses standard single-collection constraints and queries multiple vector collections.
nodes.rationale: Runs parallel queries across multiple vector collections (like islamqa and deoband) and merges results.

nodes.label: stt_service.py
nodes.description: FastAPI wrapper for faster-whisper STT service.

nodes.label: test_memory_leak.py
nodes.description: Script to test memory leak.

nodes.label: test_model_move.py
nodes.description: Tests loading and moving model to and from GPU for ParlerTTS.

nodes.label: translation_service.py
nodes.description: Standalone Python FastAPI service acting as an offline language translator using IndicTrans2.

nodes.label: tts_service.py
nodes.description: FastAPI web server for Text-To-Speech using Parler-TTS.

nodes.label: Project Architecture
nodes.description: Six-layer project architecture for Rushd-ul-Ilm app including Android UI, Backend Docker, Knowledge Ingestion, etc.

nodes.label: Three-Tier Offline Fallback
nodes.description: Fallback mechanism ensuring app works via Internet, LAN, or fully Offline.

nodes.label: AI System Prompt Rules
nodes.description: Instructions for AI coding agents to follow teaching rules, anti-hallucination rules, and UI guidelines.

nodes.label: Project Overview
nodes.description: High-level overview of the app's purpose, target audience, and technology stack choices.

nodes.label: Android App Layer
nodes.description: Details about the MVVM architecture, Jetpack Compose screens, and navigation.

nodes.label: Knowledge Ingestion
nodes.description: Process of vectorizing fatwas using paraphrase-multilingual-MiniLM and storing in Qdrant.

nodes.label: Translation Pipeline
nodes.description: Multilingual translation using IndicTrans2 models.

nodes.label: Video Database
nodes.description: Scraping, indexing, and transcribing Islamic video lectures using yt-dlp and faster-whisper.

nodes.label: Backend Docker Services
nodes.description: Docker Compose setup for FastAPI, Qdrant, Ollama, IndicTrans2, Parler-TTS, and faster-whisper.

nodes.label: Micro-Task System
nodes.description: The methodology for breaking down project phases into sprints and micro-tasks for AI execution.

nodes.label: FastAPI Service
nodes.description: Main python web application service running FastAPI on port 8000.

nodes.label: Qdrant Service
nodes.description: Vector database service container running Qdrant on port 6333.

nodes.label: IndicTrans2 Service
nodes.description: Service running the IndicTrans2 machine translation models on port 8001.

nodes.label: TTS Service
nodes.description: Service running the Indic-Parler TTS models on port 8002.

nodes.label: STT Service
nodes.description: Service running the faster-whisper STT models on port 8003.

nodes.label: whisper_jni
nodes.description: Custom JNI wrapper for the Android app linking against whisper.cpp.

nodes.label: FastAPI
nodes.description: Modern web framework for building APIs.

nodes.label: Uvicorn
nodes.description: ASGI server that runs the FastAPI application.

nodes.label: Qdrant Client
nodes.description: Client library for Qdrant vector database.

nodes.label: LlamaIndex Core
nodes.description: Core library for building RAG pipelines.

nodes.label: sentence-transformers
nodes.description: Converts text into vector embeddings.

nodes.label: IndicTransToolkit
nodes.description: Toolkit for IndicTrans2 translation models.

nodes.label: faster-whisper
nodes.description: Accelerated Whisper engine for speech-to-text.

nodes.label: namaz.mp3
nodes.description: Audio file for Namaz content.

nodes.label: namaz.wav
nodes.description: Audio file for Namaz content.

nodes.label: wazu.mp3
nodes.description: Audio file for Wazu content.

nodes.label: wazu.wav
nodes.description: Audio file for Wazu content.

nodes.label: Qwen3:4b
nodes.description: Local LLM used for generating cited answers.
nodes.rationale: Selected because it fits the 4GB VRAM limit on the developer's machine.

nodes.label: Qdrant
nodes.description: Vector database used for semantic search.

nodes.label: IndicTrans2
nodes.description: Translation engine for Telugu/Urdu to English.

nodes.label: faster-whisper-large-v3-turbo
nodes.description: Online STT engine for Telugu and Urdu.

nodes.label: Graphiti
nodes.description: Temporal knowledge graph memory for AI agents.

nodes.label: Mem0
nodes.description: Cross-session AI agent memory.

hyperedges.description: All components that make up the local Room database offline storage system
Network module and data models comprising the remote API communication layer
All Android MVVM ViewModels managing app UI state.
All FastAPI backend services in the Python server layer.
Scripts used to download AI models locally.
All AI and backend services orchestrated by Docker Compose.
